questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The value of acceleration due to gravity varies with the distance from the centre of the Earth.
questions.reason: Reason (R): According to the universal law of gravitation, the force of gravitational attraction on a body is inversely proportional to the square of the distance between the centres of the two bodies.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the variation of acceleration due to gravity with distance is indeed explained by the inverse square law of gravitational force.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The gravitational field is a vector field.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Gravitational field is defined as the gravitational force per unit mass at a point.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the gravitational field is indeed a vector field, and its definition as the gravitational force per unit mass at a point supports this fact.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The weight of an object is the same at all points on its surface.
questions.reason: Reason (R): According to the universal law of gravitation, the force of gravitational attraction on a body is proportional to its mass.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the weight of an object varies with the distance from the centre of the Earth, not because of its mass.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The gravitational force between two bodies is independent of the medium between them.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Gravitational force is an internal force of the system and does not depend on the medium between the two bodies.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the gravitational force between two bodies does indeed not depend on the medium between them, and this is explained by the fact that it is an internal force of the system.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The force of gravitation between two bodies is a conservative force.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The work done in moving a body from one point to another in a gravitational field depends only on the initial and final positions of the body.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the force of gravitation between two bodies is indeed a conservative force, and its conservative nature is explained by the fact that the work done in moving a body from one point to another in a gravitational field depends only on the initial and final positions of the body.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The value of acceleration due to gravity at the equator is less than its value at the poles.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The value of acceleration due to gravity varies with the distance from the centre of the Earth.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the value of acceleration due to gravity is indeed less at the equator than at the poles, and this is explained by the fact that the distance from the centre of the Earth is greater at the equator.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The gravitational force between two bodies is a non-central force.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The gravitational force acts along the line joining the centres of the two bodies.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the gravitational force between two bodies is indeed a central force, not a non-central force, and its central nature is explained by the fact that it acts along the line joining the centres of the two bodies.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The weight of a body is independent of its mass.
questions.reason: Reason (R): According to the universal law of gravitation, the force of gravitational attraction on a body is proportional to its mass.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The gravitational potential at the surface of the Earth is zero.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The gravitational potential is defined as the work done in bringing a body from infinity to a point in the gravitational field.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the gravitational potential at the surface of the Earth is indeed not zero, and this is explained by the fact that the work done in bringing a body from infinity to a point in the gravitational field is non-zero.
